data.title: Assessing Maplebear (CART) Valuation After Recent Share Price Pullback And Earnings Figures
data.description:
Advertisement

What recent performance suggests about Maplebear (CART)

With no single headline event driving attention today, Maplebear (CART) is drawing interest as investors reassess its recent share price performance alongside its latest reported revenue and net income figures.

See our latest analysis for Maplebear.

The recent pullback, including a 1 day share price return of 2.57% decline and a 7 day share price return of 7.48% decline, follows a 90 day share price return of 3.43% alongside a 1 year total shareholder return of 14.53% decline. This suggests earlier momentum has cooled as investors reassess growth potential and risks around the current US$39.81 level.

If you are comparing Maplebear with other tech driven growth stories, this could be a good moment to broaden your search with high growth tech and AI stocks.

With revenue of US$3,633.0m, net income of US$505.0m and a reported intrinsic discount of 60.85%, is Maplebear quietly offering value here, or is the current price already accounting for much of its future growth?

Most Popular Narrative: 21.8% Undervalued

The most followed narrative pegs Maplebear's fair value at roughly US$50.92 per share, comfortably above the recent US$39.81 close, framing the stock as undervalued on those assumptions.

Deepening enterprise partnerships and a growing suite of omnichannel retailer integrations (such as Storefront, Carrot Ads, Caper Carts, Carrot Tags) are increasing stickiness with major retail chains, creating new recurring revenue streams and driving higher margin, non transaction based revenues (e.g., advertising, in store tech), making the business model less volatile and supporting sustainable margin expansion and earnings resilience.

Read the complete narrative.

Curious how this recurring revenue push, margin outlook, and future P/E assumption come together into that fair value number? The full narrative lays out the exact growth, profitability, and valuation path behind this call.

Result: Fair Value of $50.92 (UNDERVALUED)

Have a read of the narrative in full and understand what's behind the forecasts.

However, you still need to factor in risks such as higher labor and regulatory costs, tougher competition, and partner renegotiations that could pressure margins and revenue.

Find out about the key risks to this Maplebear narrative.

Another Angle: P/E Ratios Tell A Different Story

Those fair value models suggest upside, but the plain P/E comparison looks more mixed. At 20.7x earnings, Maplebear trades slightly below the US Consumer Retailing average of 21.8x and well below a 28.8x peer average, yet above its own fair ratio of 19x, which hints at some valuation risk if sentiment cools.

See what the numbers say about this price — find out in our valuation breakdown.

data.title: A Look At UDR’s (UDR) Valuation After Governance Updates And Multifamily Growth Moves
data.description:
UDR (UDR) shares are in focus after the company appointed veteran auditor Ellen M. Goitia as an independent director, expanding the board to ten members and adding experience in both governance and risk oversight.

See our latest analysis for UDR.

UDR’s latest share price of $37.08 comes after a 1 month share price return of 4.57% and a 3 month gain of 2.74%. The 1 year total shareholder return of a 6.31% decline contrasts with a 5 year total shareholder return of 14.44%, suggesting that near term momentum is picking up even as longer term compounding has been more modest.

If this kind of governance and risk focus interests you, it could be a good moment to broaden your search with healthcare stocks.

With UDR trading at $37.08, sitting on a 34% intrinsic discount and below the average analyst price target, the key question is whether investors are seeing an undervalued apartment REIT or a stock that already reflects its future growth potential.

Advertisement

Most Popular Narrative: 7.9% Undervalued

Compared with UDR's last close of US$37.08, the most followed narrative points to a fair value of about US$40.25, which frames the current discount as modest but meaningful.

The analysts have a consensus price target of $44.119 for UDR based on their expectations of its future earnings growth, profit margins and other risk factors. However, there is a degree of disagreement amongst analysts, with the most bullish reporting a price target of $49.0, and the most bearish reporting a price target of just $37.0.

Read the complete narrative.

Curious what earnings path and margin profile could support a P/E multiple far above the broader Residential REITs group, even after using a 7% plus discount rate?

Result: Fair Value of $40.25 (UNDERVALUED)

Have a read of the narrative in full and understand what's behind the forecasts.

However, softer apartment fundamentals and persistent regulatory risk in key coastal markets could pressure rent growth and margins, which challenges the idea that UDR’s current discount is attractive.

Find out about the key risks to this UDR narrative.

Another View: Multiples Flash A Caution Sign

Our fair value work suggests UDR trades at a discount, yet its P/E of 84.6x is far above both peers at 27.8x and the Residential REITs industry at 25.1x, and well above a fair ratio of 33.3x. That gap points to valuation risk if sentiment cools.

See what the numbers say about this price — find out in our valuation breakdown.

data.description:

data.title: Arrowhead Pharmaceuticals (ARWR) Valuation After REDEMPLO Approvals And New Obesity Trial Data
data.description:
Arrowhead Pharmaceuticals (ARWR) has just combined a third major approval for REDEMPLO (plozasiran) with fresh obesity trial data and sizeable new financings, giving investors several material updates to digest at once.

See our latest analysis for Arrowhead Pharmaceuticals.

These approvals and obesity trial updates come after a sharp 79.98% 3 month share price return and a very large 1 year total shareholder return of 248.32%. However, the year to date share price return of a 2.27% decline suggests momentum has cooled slightly after a strong run into recent news.

If this kind of cardiometabolic and obesity pipeline interests you, it could be a good moment to scan other healthcare stocks that might be riding similar treatment themes.

With Arrowhead now carrying three major approvals for REDEMPLO, fresh obesity data, and roughly US$825 million of recent financing behind it, is a US$66 share price still underestimating what comes next, or is future growth already fully priced in?

Advertisement

Most Popular Narrative: 3% Overvalued

At a last close of US$66.25 against a narrative fair value of about US$64.08, Arrowhead is framed as slightly ahead of that central estimate, with the story leaning heavily on future cardiometabolic and RNAi opportunities.

Arrowhead's advancing late-stage clinical pipeline, especially the expected launch of plozasiran for FCS and SHTG, plus pivotal studies for three other RNAi therapies targeting major unmet needs, positions the company to capitalize on rising demand for therapies driven by an aging population and increasing prevalence of chronic and genetic diseases, which could significantly accelerate revenue growth as approvals and launches materialize.

Read the complete narrative.

Curious how this story supports a premium valuation? The key ingredients are future margins, revenue resets, and an earnings multiple rarely seen outside fast growing sectors.

Result: Fair Value of $64.08 (OVERVALUED)

Have a read of the narrative in full and understand what's behind the forecasts.

However, this story still hinges on successful late stage trial readouts and partner follow through. As a result, any clinical or collaboration setbacks could quickly challenge today’s premium.

data.description:

  • This combination of tighter cost control, clearer medium‑term financial targets, and advancing mRNA pipeline has prompted analysts and investors to reassess Moderna’s business outlook more favorably.

The updated 2025 revenue outlook and expense cuts support the key near term catalyst, which is progress and potential approvals in late stage respiratory and cancer programs, but they do not remove the biggest risk right now: continued losses and uncertainty around reaching cash breakeven by 2028.

Among recent announcements, the company’s expectation of about US$1.9 billion in 2025 revenue alongside meaningful operating expense reductions stands out, as it tightens the link between pipeline execution and the cash breakeven goal. For investors focused on catalysts, this guidance frames how upcoming data readouts and potential approvals in flu, combination vaccines, oncology and rare diseases might influence the path to a more diversified, less seasonal revenue base.

data.title: Lithium Americas (TSX:LAC) Valuation After Thacker Pass Progress And New Technology Partnerships
data.description:
Recent progress at Lithium Americas (TSX:LAC) Thacker Pass project and new technology focused partnerships has drawn fresh attention, as investors weigh these developments against strong lithium demand and the company’s ongoing financial challenges.

See our latest analysis for Lithium Americas.

Those Thacker Pass milestones and new technology partnerships have arrived alongside a 24.43% year to date share price return and a 64.65% total shareholder return over the past year. The recent 23.48% 1 month share price gain has been partially offset by a 14.48% 3 month decline, suggesting momentum has picked up again as investors reassess Lithium Americas around key supply chain and regulatory updates.

If this lithium story has your attention, it could be worth seeing what else is moving in materials and energy, starting with fast growing stocks with high insider ownership.

So with Lithium Americas posting strong recent returns, no current revenue, and a CA$8.15 share price that sits above the average analyst target, is the market overexcited here or still underestimating any future growth?

Advertisement

Price to Book of 3.7x: Is It Justified?

Lithium Americas trades on a P/B of 3.7x, and with the last close at CA$8.15, that multiple matters if you are benchmarking it against peers.

P/B compares the company’s market value to its net assets. This is a common reference point for pre revenue or early stage resource names where earnings do not yet tell the story.

For Lithium Americas, this 3.7x P/B is described as expensive against the broader Canadian Metals and Mining industry average of 3.4x. It is also flagged as good value relative to a much higher peer group average of 17x. The same 3.7x figure can therefore look rich or restrained depending on which comparison set you care about most.

What Is StubHub Holdings' Investment Narrative?

For StubHub, the big-picture belief is that its ticketing marketplace can translate rising gross merchandise sales and new partnerships into a path toward sustainable profitability, despite sizable recent losses and share price volatility. The RPM Capital Partners stake adds another data point that some institutions view the current risk‑reward as attractive, but it does not fundamentally change the near term catalysts, which still center on execution in live events, integration of new products like the ChatGPT app experience, and monetization of direct issuance partnerships. The more immediate tension remains between upbeat revenue trends and the legal overhang from the shareholder class action, as well as questions around governance and high executive pay relative to performance. The new position underscores interest, but it does not mute these risks.

HNI (HNI) plans to close its Wayland, New York, manufacturing plant in 2027, consolidating production into other North American facilities and targeting annual cost savings of about US$7.5 million to US$8.0 million.

The move is tied to the integration of Kimball International, with total cost synergies now anticipated at US$68 million by the end of 2028 and expected pre tax consolidation charges of US$14.9 million across 2026 and 2027.

data.title: Is It Time To Revisit Harrow (HROW) After Its Strong Multi Year Share Price Run?
data.description:
• If you are wondering whether Harrow's share price still makes sense after recent moves, you are not alone and the numbers give us plenty to talk about.
  • The stock trades at US$48.49, with a 7 day return of a 5% decline, a 30 day return of 8.3% and a 1 year return of 44.9%, while the 3 year return is very large and the 5 year return is also very large.
  • Recent news flow around Harrow has mainly focused on its position in the pharmaceuticals and biotech space and investor interest in how its current product portfolio and pipeline could influence future performance. These themes help frame the recent share price swings as the market weighs potential growth against the usual sector risks.
  • Our Simply Wall St valuation model currently gives Harrow a valuation score of 5 out of 6. This suggests it screens as undervalued on most of the checks we run. Next we will look at how different valuation approaches treat the stock before closing with an even more practical way to think about its value. Stay updated when valuation signals shift by adding Harrow to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our screener to discover other companies that fit your criteria.

Harrow delivered 44.9% returns over the last year. See how this stacks up to the rest of the Pharmaceuticals industry.

Advertisement

Approach 1: Harrow Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model takes estimates of a company’s future cash flows and discounts them back to today’s dollars to arrive at an intrinsic value per share.

For Harrow, the model used is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach. The latest twelve month free cash flow is a loss of $75.15 million, so the valuation leans heavily on future projections rather than current cash generation.

Analysts and internal estimates project free cash flow rising to $416.40 million in 2030, with intermediate projections between 2026 and 2035 ranging from $81.70 million to $660.05 million. Simply Wall St uses analyst estimates where available and then extrapolates the later years to complete the 10 year cash flow path.

Discounting these projected cash flows back to today results in an estimated intrinsic value of about $324.20 per share. Compared with the recent share price of $48.49, the model implies Harrow trades at roughly an 85.0% discount. This points to the stock screening as significantly undervalued on this measure.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests Harrow is undervalued by 85.0%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 868 more undervalued stocks based on cash flows.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Harrow.

Approach 2: Harrow Price vs Sales

For companies that are not yet consistently profitable, the P/S ratio is often a useful yardstick because it compares what investors are paying to the revenue the business is already generating, without relying on earnings that can be negative or volatile.

Growth expectations and risk still matter because a higher growth outlook or lower perceived risk can justify a higher “normal” P/S multiple, while slower expected growth or higher risk usually supports a lower one. Context is therefore important when interpreting any single number.

Harrow currently trades on a P/S of 7.18x, compared with the Pharmaceuticals industry average of 4.81x and a peer group average of 8.22x. Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio for Harrow is 8.72x, which is its proprietary view of what the P/S should be given factors such as earnings growth profile, industry, profit margins, market cap and company specific risks.

This Fair Ratio is often more informative than a simple peer or industry comparison because it adjusts for Harrow’s own growth, risk and profitability characteristics rather than assuming all companies deserve the same multiple. On this basis, Harrow’s actual P/S of 7.18x sits below the Fair Ratio of 8.72x, which suggests the shares screen as undervalued on this measure.

Result: UNDERVALUED

P/S ratios tell one story, but what if the real opportunity lies elsewhere? Discover 1442 companies where insiders are betting big on explosive growth.

Upgrade Your Decision Making: Choose your Harrow Narrative

Earlier we mentioned that there is an even better way to understand valuation. On Simply Wall St’s Community page you can use Narratives, where you set out your own story for Harrow by linking assumptions about future revenue, earnings and margins to a forecast. You can then compare the Fair Value that results from that story to the current price to help decide if you want to buy or sell. You can also see that Narrative update as new news or earnings arrive, with different investors sometimes landing far apart. For example, one Narrative might support a Fair Value near the higher end of recent targets around US$94, while a more cautious view could sit closer to US$42.

Do you think there's more to the story for Harrow? Head over to our Community to see what others are saying!

data.description:

See our latest analysis for Melco Resorts & Entertainment.

At a share price of $6.73, Melco’s recent 30 day share price return of 14.59% and year to date share price return of 10.39% contrast with a 1 year total shareholder return of 23.49%. The 3 and 5 year total shareholder returns of 48.31% and 60.13% highlight how earlier losses still weigh on long term performance and suggest that recent analyst caution around Macau and Melco’s margin outlook is arriving just as shorter term momentum has been fading again.

If analyst downgrades and sector wide Macau questions have you reassessing your watchlist, it could be a useful moment to look across aerospace and defense stocks as a very different corner of the market.

With MLCO trading at $6.73, a value score of 4 and a wide gap to the average analyst target, investors have to ask whether this discount points to mispricing or if the market is already bracing for weaker growth.

Price-to-Earnings of 25.2x: Is it justified?

At a last close of $6.73, Melco Resorts & Entertainment trades on a P/E of 25.2x, which analysts flag as good value versus some peers but expensive against the broader US Hospitality group.

The P/E multiple compares the current share price with earnings per share, so it effectively shows how much investors are paying for each dollar of current profit. For a casino and resort operator like Melco, where profitability has only recently returned, this multiple can also reflect how the market weighs fresh earnings against a long stretch of earlier losses.

Our data suggests the current P/E sits below an estimated fair P/E of 27.1x. This signals the market is placing a slightly lower value on Melco’s earnings than the level implied by the SWS fair ratio model. That fair ratio level is a reference point the valuation could gravitate toward if sentiment and fundamentals stay aligned with the framework behind that estimate.

Compared with its direct peer group, Melco’s 25.2x P/E screens as good value versus a peer average of 40.1x. However, it is still described as expensive against the US Hospitality industry average of 21.4x. That split paints a picture of a stock priced richer than the wider sector, but cheaper than closer comparables, leaving room for readers to judge whether the peer premium or the sector discount carries more weight.

The Actable results reinforce the AI and analytics catalyst in the near term, but do not materially change the biggest risk right now: ongoing regulatory and privacy pressures on how data is collected, linked, and monetized.

The recent hire of Francesca Noli to lead Global Consumer Solutions is particularly relevant here, because it underlines TransUnion’s push to turn its data and AI capabilities into more consumer-facing products like TruEmpower. If management executes, this could complement TruAudience and identity/fraud solutions as key earnings growth drivers, even as investors watch for rising compliance and modernization costs.

Yet investors should also be aware that tighter data privacy rules or a major cyber incident could quickly change how sustainably this model scales...

data.title: How Qnity Electronics’ CFO Transition And Reaffirmed 2025 Guidance At Q (Q) Has Changed Its Investment Story
data.description:
• Qnity Electronics, Inc. recently announced that Chief Financial Officer Matt Harbaugh has stepped down for health reasons, appointing current Principal Accounting Officer and Controller Mike Goss as Interim CFO while it conducts a comprehensive search for a permanent successor and reaffirming its full-year 2025 net sales guidance of approximately US$4.7 billion.
  • The choice of an interim CFO with nearly three decades of finance experience, including senior roles at DuPont and Ernst & Young, underscores Qnity’s emphasis on continuity in financial oversight during this leadership transition.
  • We’ll now examine how reaffirmed guidance alongside Mike Goss’s appointment as interim CFO shapes Qnity Electronics’ broader investment narrative.

Outshine the giants: these 24 early-stage AI stocks could fund your retirement.

Advertisement

What Is Qnity Electronics' Investment Narrative?

For Qnity Electronics, the core investment case still comes down to believing in a focused, stand-alone electronics materials business that can convert its US$4.7 billion net sales outlook into resilient, high-quality earnings. The recent CFO change introduces a governance wrinkle just months after the DuPont spin-off, but the board’s decision to appoint long-time Controller and Principal Accounting Officer Mike Goss as interim CFO, while reaffirming 2025 guidance, suggests limited immediate disruption to near-term catalysts such as index inclusion flows, execution on long-term supply agreements and early capital allocation decisions. The bigger risks remain around Qnity’s ability to sustain its margin profile and return on equity while carrying its new post-separation capital structure, particularly with a relatively new board still bedding down oversight. This leadership transition now sits squarely inside that risk discussion.

Qnity Electronics' shares are on the way up, but could they be overextended? Uncover how much higher they are than fair value.

Exploring Other Perspectives

Two fair value estimates from the Simply Wall St Community span roughly US$56 to US$105 per share, showing how differently individual investors are modelling Qnity’s prospects. Set against a recent leadership change in the finance function and a still-fresh spin-off track record, this spread highlights why you may want to weigh multiple viewpoints before forming a view on how reliably Qnity can execute on its current guidance.

data.title: How Euronext’s On-Chain Repo Trials (ENXTPA:ENX) Are Quietly Reframing Its Market Infrastructure Ambitions
data.description:
• A consortium of market infrastructure and trading firms recently completed a third wave of transactions on the Canton Network, using tokenized commercial bank deposits via LSEG Digital Settlement House and tokenized collateral such as European government bonds and U.S. Treasuries for on-chain intraday repo across multiple currencies and borders.
  • Euronext highlighted that this work is part of a broader industry push to build tokenization and on-chain infrastructure while staying aligned with regulated market requirements, underscoring how exchange operators are testing new settlement rails without stepping outside existing rulebooks.
  • We’ll now examine how Euronext’s on-chain repo participation and tokenization focus may influence its existing investment narrative and outlook.

Trump's oil boom is here - pipelines are primed to profit. Discover the 22 US stocks riding the wave.

Advertisement

Euronext Investment Narrative Recap

To own Euronext, you need to believe it can keep compounding earnings by broadening beyond pure trading volumes while managing integration and regulatory headwinds. The Canton Network on-chain repo tests support this diversification angle but do not materially change the near term focus on volume normalization risk and the share buyback plus potential CAC 40 inclusion as key sentiment drivers.

The most relevant recent development is Euronext’s ongoing acquisition of the Athens Stock Exchange (ATHEX), which expands its clearing, settlement and listing footprint. Taken together with its tokenization experiments, this points investors toward a broader market infrastructure story where execution risk around integration and returns on new platforms sits alongside the potential uplift from index inclusion and capital returns.

Yet while these initiatives look promising, investors should be aware that revenue tied to trading activity could still...
